content.content:
1	import * as approvalsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/approvals"
2	import * as extensionsRepo from "~~/server/repositories/extensions"
3	import * as reviewersRepo from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
4	import {
5	  decideApprovalOutcome,
6	  decideWithdrawOutcome,
7	  type ApprovalAction,
8	  type OfficialTier,
9	} from "~~/shared/approvals/state"
10	
11	import { useDb } from "./db"
12	import { safeSend } from "./inngest"
13	
14	// Orchestrators over the approvals/reviewers/extensions repositories. Pure
15	// transitions live in `shared/approvals/state.ts`; this file loads rows,
16	// opens transactions, fires Inngest events, and maps domain errors to
17	// HTTP-shaped codes the endpoints translate.
18	
19	export type ApprovalErrorCode =
20	  | "extension_not_found"
21	  | "not_publisher_owner"
22	  | "extension_not_published"
23	  | "request_not_found"
24	  | "request_not_pending"
25	  | "duplicate_pending_request"
26	  | "not_reviewer"
27	  | "not_requester"
28	  | "missing_product_line"
29	  | "unexpected_product_line"
30	  | "extension_not_official"
31	
32	export class ApprovalError extends Error {
33	  readonly code: ApprovalErrorCode
34	  constructor(code: ApprovalErrorCode) {
35	    super(code)
36	    this.code = code
37	    this.name = "ApprovalError"
38	  }
39	}
40	
41	export interface SubmitParams {
42	  extensionId: string
43	  requestedTier: OfficialTier
44	  subCat: string
45	  productLineId: string | null
46	  userId: string
47	  reason: string | undefined
48	}
49	
50	export async function submitRequest(
51	  params: SubmitParams,
52	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow> {
53	  // Shape rule mirrors the DB CHECK: productLineId is present iff the
54	  // requested tier is `productLine`. Surfacing both error codes lets the
55	  // endpoint give a precise message instead of bouncing the raw 23514.
56	  if (params.requestedTier === "productLine" && !params.productLineId) {
57	    throw new ApprovalError("missing_product_line")
58	  }
59	  if (params.requestedTier === "company" && params.productLineId) {
60	    throw new ApprovalError("unexpected_product_line")
61	  }
62	
63	  const db = useDb()
64	
65	  const ext = await extensionsRepo.findById(db, params.extensionId)
66	  if (!ext) throw new ApprovalError("extension_not_found")
67	  if (ext.publisherUserId !== params.userId) {
68	    throw new ApprovalError("not_publisher_owner")
69	  }
70	  if (ext.visibility !== "published") {
71	    throw new ApprovalError("extension_not_published")
72	  }
73	
74	  const row = await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
75	    // Re-check inside the transaction — race window between the visibility
76	    // load above and the insert below would otherwise let a duplicate slip
77	    // through. PG isolation is `read committed` so the second check still
78	    // sees a concurrent insert that already committed.
79	    const existing = await approvalsRepo.findPendingByExtension(
80	      tx,
81	      params.extensionId,
82	    )
83	    if (existing) throw new ApprovalError("duplicate_pending_request")
84	
85	    return approvalsRepo.insertRequest(tx, {
86	      id: crypto.randomUUID(),
87	      extensionId: params.extensionId,
88	      requestedTier: params.requestedTier,
89	      subCat: params.subCat,
90	      productLineId: params.productLineId,
91	      requestedByUserId: params.userId,
92	      reason: params.reason ?? null,
93	    })
94	  })
95	
96	  // Best-effort: the request row is the source of truth and is already
97	  // visible in the reviewer queue. A transient Inngest outage (or, in dev,
98	  // a missing `inngest-cli dev` process) must not fail the user's submit
99	  // after the row has been committed.
100	  await safeSend({
101	    name: "extension/approval.requested",
102	    data: {
103	      requestId: row.id,
104	      extensionId: row.extensionId,
105	      requestedTier: row.requestedTier,
106	      subCat: row.subCat,
107	      productLineId: row.productLineId,
108	      requesterUserId: row.requestedByUserId,
109	    },
110	  })
111	
112	  return row
113	}
114	
115	export interface DecideParams {
116	  requestId: string
117	  action: ApprovalAction
118	  reviewerUserId: string
119	}
120	
121	export async function decideRequest(
122	  params: DecideParams,
123	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow> {
124	  const db = useDb()
125	
126	  const current = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
127	  if (!current) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_found")
128	  if (current.status !== "pending") {
129	    throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
130	  }
131	
132	  // Super-admins can decide on any cell; otherwise the reviewer must be
133	  // assigned to the exact (requestedTier, subCat, productLineId?) cell this
134	  // request was filed against. Tier mismatches don't leak across cells.
135	  const allowed =
136	    (await reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin(db, params.reviewerUserId)) ||
137	    (await approvalsRepo.isReviewerForCell(
138	      db,
139	      params.reviewerUserId,
140	      current.requestedTier,
141	      current.subCat,
142	      current.productLineId,
143	    ))
144	  if (!allowed) throw new ApprovalError("not_reviewer")
145	
146	  const outcome = decideApprovalOutcome({
147	    current: {
148	      status: current.status,
149	      requestedTier: current.requestedTier,
150	    },
151	    action: params.action,
152	    reviewerUserId: params.reviewerUserId,
153	    now: new Date(),
154	  })
155	
156	  // Optimistic locking lives inside the repository's UPDATE — if a second
157	  // reviewer decided between the pre-check above and this transaction, the
158	  // affected row count comes back zero and we surface the race as
159	  // `request_not_pending`.
160	  await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
161	    const updated = await approvalsRepo.applyDecision(
162	      tx,
163	      params.requestId,
164	      outcome.request,
165	    )
166	    if (updated === 0) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
167	    if (outcome.extension) {
168	      await approvalsRepo.setExtensionOfficialTier(
169	        tx,
170	        current.extensionId,
171	        outcome.extension.officialTier,
172	        current.productLineId,
173	      )
174	    }
175	  })
176	
177	  const updated = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
178	  if (!updated) throw new Error("approval request vanished mid-decide")
179	
180	  // Best-effort notification after commit; see submitRequest for rationale.
181	  await safeSend({
182	    name: "extension/approval.decided",
183	    data: {
184	      requestId: updated.id,
185	      extensionId: updated.extensionId,
186	      decision: outcome.request.status,
187	      decidedByUserId: params.reviewerUserId,
188	    },
189	  })
190	
191	  return updated
192	}
193	
194	export interface WithdrawParams {
195	  requestId: string
196	  userId: string
197	}
198	
199	export async function withdrawRequest(
200	  params: WithdrawParams,
201	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow> {
202	  const db = useDb()
203	
204	  const current = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
205	  if (!current) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_found")
206	  if (current.requestedByUserId !== params.userId) {
207	    throw new ApprovalError("not_requester")
208	  }
209	  if (current.status !== "pending") {
210	    throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
211	  }
212	
213	  const outcome = decideWithdrawOutcome({
214	    current: { status: current.status },
215	    now: new Date(),
216	  })
217	  const affected = await approvalsRepo.applyWithdraw(
218	    db,
219	    params.requestId,
220	    outcome.request.decidedAt,
221	  )
222	  if (affected === 0) throw new ApprovalError("request_not_pending")
223	
224	  const updated = await approvalsRepo.findById(db, params.requestId)
225	  if (!updated) throw new Error("approval request vanished mid-withdraw")
226	  return updated
227	}
228	
229	export interface RevokeTierParams {
230	  extensionId: string
231	  superAdminUserId: string
232	  note: string
233	}
234	
235	export interface RevokeTierResult {
236	  extensionId: string
237	  revokedAt: Date
238	}
239	
240	// Super-admin Revoke action from the detail page. Loads the extension,
241	// pre-checks that it's actually Official, then atomically clears tier +
242	// productLineId and writes the audit trio. The DB-level WHERE clause in
243	// applyRevocation re-checks officialTier IS NOT NULL so a concurrent
244	// revoke / re-approval loses the race and surfaces as
245	// `extension_not_official` rather than silently clobbering the row.
246	// Caller is expected to already be a super-admin (gated at the endpoint
247	// via requireSuperAdmin) — we don't re-check here for the same reason the
248	// reviewer-assign endpoint doesn't re-check its gate.
249	export async function revokeTier(
250	  params: RevokeTierParams,
251	): Promise<RevokeTierResult> {
252	  const db = useDb()
253	  const ext = await extensionsRepo.findById(db, params.extensionId)
254	  if (!ext) throw new ApprovalError("extension_not_found")
255	  if (!ext.officialTier) throw new ApprovalError("extension_not_official")
256	
257	  const revokedAt = new Date()
258	  const affected = await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
259	    return approvalsRepo.applyRevocation(tx, params.extensionId, {
260	      revokedAt,
261	      revokedByUserId: params.superAdminUserId,
262	      revocationNote: params.note,
263	    })
264	  })
265	  if (affected === 0) throw new ApprovalError("extension_not_official")
266	
267	  // Best-effort notification after commit; see submitRequest for rationale.
268	  await safeSend({
269	    name: "extension/tier.revoked",
270	    data: {
271	      extensionId: params.extensionId,
272	      revokedByUserId: params.superAdminUserId,
273	      revokedAt: revokedAt.toISOString(),
274	      note: params.note,
275	    },
276	  })
277	
278	  return { extensionId: params.extensionId, revokedAt }
279	}
280	
281	export interface ReviewerQueueFilters {
282	  tier?: OfficialTier
283	  subCat?: string
284	  productLineId?: string
285	}
286	
287	// Reviewer queue — pending requests in any cell this user covers. Returns
288	// an empty array when the user has no cell assignments (and is not a
289	// super-admin); super-admins see the whole pending queue.
290	//
291	// Optional `filters` narrow the queue without changing the trust model:
292	// the cells are computed first (so a reviewer never queries outside their
293	// own assignment), then filtered, then handed to listPendingForCells. A
294	// filter that excludes every cell yields an empty `cells` array, which
295	// listPendingForCells short-circuits to [] — no special-case branch.
296	export async function listReviewerQueue(
297	  userId: string,
298	  filters: ReviewerQueueFilters = {},
299	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
300	  const db = useDb()
301	  let cells: Array<{
302	    tier: OfficialTier
303	    subCat: string
304	    productLineId: string | null
305	  }>
306	  if (await reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin(db, userId)) {
307	    // Treat super-admin as "assigned to every cell". Pulled from the matrix
308	    // verbatim so an empty matrix still surfaces no rows — better than a
309	    // silent firehose. Productline IS NULL is encoded as the literal '∅' in
310	    // the dedup key so a (company, cloud, null) cell doesn't collapse onto
311	    // (productLine, cloud, '') by accident.
312	    cells = await reviewersRepo
313	      .listMatrix(db)
314	      .then((rows) =>
315	        Array.from(
316	          new Set(
317	            rows.map((r) => `${r.tier}:${r.subCat}:${r.productLineId ?? "∅"}`),
318	          ),
319	        ).map((k) => {
320	          const [tier, subCat, pl] = k.split(":") as [OfficialTier, string, string]
321	          return {
322	            tier,
323	            subCat,
324	            productLineId: pl === "∅" ? null : pl,
325	          }
326	        }),
327	      )
328	  } else {
329	    cells = await reviewersRepo.listCellsForUser(db, userId)
330	  }
331	
332	  const narrowed = cells.filter((c) => {
333	    if (filters.tier && c.tier !== filters.tier) return false
334	    if (filters.subCat && c.subCat !== filters.subCat) return false
335	    if (filters.productLineId && c.productLineId !== filters.productLineId)
336	      return false
337	    return true
338	  })
339	  return approvalsRepo.listPendingForCells(db, narrowed)
340	}
341	
342	export async function listPublisherRequests(
343	  userId: string,
344	): Promise<approvalsRepo.ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
345	  return approvalsRepo.listForPublisher(useDb(), userId)
346	}

1	import { and, desc, eq, inArray, isNull, sql } from "drizzle-orm"
2	
3	import type {
4	  ApprovalStatus,
5	  OfficialTier,
6	} from "~~/shared/approvals/state"
7	import {
8	  approvalRequests,
9	  approvalReviewers,
10	  extensions,
11	} from "~~/shared/db/schema"
12	
13	import type { Transactable } from "./types"
14	
15	// `approval_requests` table accessor. Used by the approval orchestrator to
16	// drive the publisher submit / reviewer decide / publisher withdraw flow.
17	// `ApprovalStatus` and `OfficialTier` are re-exported so callers that
18	// already depend on the repository module don't need a second import.
19	
20	export type { ApprovalStatus, OfficialTier }
21	
22	export interface ApprovalRequestRow {
23	  id: string
24	  extensionId: string
25	  requestedTier: OfficialTier
26	  subCat: string
27	  productLineId: string | null
28	  requestedByUserId: string
29	  reason: string | null
30	  status: ApprovalStatus
31	  decidedByUserId: string | null
32	  decidedAt: Date | null
33	  reviewerNote: string | null
34	  createdAt: Date
35	  updatedAt: Date
36	}
37	
38	const fullSelect = {
39	  id: approvalRequests.id,
40	  extensionId: approvalRequests.extensionId,
41	  requestedTier: approvalRequests.requestedTier,
42	  subCat: approvalRequests.subCat,
43	  productLineId: approvalRequests.productLineId,
44	  requestedByUserId: approvalRequests.requestedByUserId,
45	  reason: approvalRequests.reason,
46	  status: approvalRequests.status,
47	  decidedByUserId: approvalRequests.decidedByUserId,
48	  decidedAt: approvalRequests.decidedAt,
49	  reviewerNote: approvalRequests.reviewerNote,
50	  createdAt: approvalRequests.createdAt,
51	  updatedAt: approvalRequests.updatedAt,
52	}
53	
54	export interface InsertApprovalRequest {
55	  id: string
56	  extensionId: string
57	  requestedTier: OfficialTier
58	  subCat: string
59	  productLineId: string | null
60	  requestedByUserId: string
61	  reason: string | null
62	}
63	
64	export async function insertRequest(
65	  db: Transactable,
66	  row: InsertApprovalRequest,
67	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow> {
68	  const [created] = await db
69	    .insert(approvalRequests)
70	    .values(row)
71	    .returning(fullSelect)
72	  if (!created) throw new Error("approval request insert vanished")
73	  return created
74	}
75	
76	export async function findById(
77	  db: Transactable,
78	  id: string,
79	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow | null> {
80	  const [row] = await db
81	    .select(fullSelect)
82	    .from(approvalRequests)
83	    .where(eq(approvalRequests.id, id))
84	    .limit(1)
85	  return row ?? null
86	}
87	
88	// Bulk variant of `findPendingByExtension` for the publisher dashboard —
89	// the listing already loads many extensions, so we fan in a single query
90	// rather than N round-trips and return a Map<extId, pendingRequest>.
91	export async function findPendingForExtensions(
92	  db: Transactable,
93	  extensionIds: string[],
94	): Promise<Map<string, ApprovalRequestRow>> {
95	  if (extensionIds.length === 0) return new Map()
96	  const rows = await db
97	    .select(fullSelect)
98	    .from(approvalRequests)
99	    .where(
100	      and(
101	        inArray(approvalRequests.extensionId, extensionIds),
102	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
103	      ),
104	    )
105	  return new Map(rows.map((r) => [r.extensionId, r]))
106	}
107	
108	// Used by the at-most-one-pending guard in the submit orchestrator.
109	export async function findPendingByExtension(
110	  db: Transactable,
111	  extensionId: string,
112	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow | null> {
113	  const [row] = await db
114	    .select(fullSelect)
115	    .from(approvalRequests)
116	    .where(
117	      and(
118	        eq(approvalRequests.extensionId, extensionId),
119	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
120	      ),
121	    )
122	    .limit(1)
123	  return row ?? null
124	}
125	
126	// Publisher's "my requests" view.
127	export async function listForPublisher(
128	  db: Transactable,
129	  userId: string,
130	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
131	  return db
132	    .select(fullSelect)
133	    .from(approvalRequests)
134	    .where(eq(approvalRequests.requestedByUserId, userId))
135	    .orderBy(desc(approvalRequests.createdAt))
136	}
137	
138	// Reviewer's queue — pending requests in any (tier, subCat, productLineId?)
139	// cell that the reviewer is assigned to. Implemented as a single query
140	// against the cells the reviewer covers; the orchestrator pre-computes the
141	// cell list from `approval_reviewers` to keep this method driver-portable
142	// (no LATERAL). ProductLineId is matched IS NULL for company-tier cells
143	// and = productLineId for productLine-tier cells, so a company-tier
144	// reviewer never sees productLine-tier requests and vice versa.
145	export async function listPendingForCells(
146	  db: Transactable,
147	  cells: Array<{ tier: OfficialTier; subCat: string; productLineId: string | null }>,
148	): Promise<ApprovalRequestRow[]> {
149	  if (cells.length === 0) return []
150	  const cellMatches = cells.map((c) =>
151	    and(
152	      eq(approvalRequests.requestedTier, c.tier),
153	      eq(approvalRequests.subCat, c.subCat),
154	      c.productLineId === null
155	        ? isNull(approvalRequests.productLineId)
156	        : eq(approvalRequests.productLineId, c.productLineId),
157	    ),
158	  )
159	  return db
160	    .select(fullSelect)
161	    .from(approvalRequests)
162	    .where(
163	      and(
164	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
165	        sql`(${sql.join(cellMatches, sql` OR `)})`,
166	      ),
167	    )
168	    .orderBy(desc(approvalRequests.createdAt))
169	}
170	
171	export interface DecidePatch {
172	  status: "approved" | "rejected"
173	  decidedByUserId: string
174	  decidedAt: Date
175	  reviewerNote: string | null
176	}
177	
178	// Optimistic-locking variant: only updates a request that's still pending.
179	// Returns the number of affected rows so the orchestrator can detect a
180	// race (a second reviewer that committed between the orchestrator's
181	// pre-check and this UPDATE) and surface it as `request_not_pending`.
182	export async function applyDecision(
183	  db: Transactable,
184	  id: string,
185	  patch: DecidePatch,
186	): Promise<number> {
187	  const updated = await db
188	    .update(approvalRequests)
189	    .set({ ...patch, updatedAt: new Date() })
190	    .where(
191	      and(
192	        eq(approvalRequests.id, id),
193	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
194	      ),
195	    )
196	    .returning({ id: approvalRequests.id })
197	  return updated.length
198	}
199	
200	// Same optimistic-locking shape as `applyDecision` — only a pending
201	// request can be withdrawn, and the affected row count tells the caller
202	// whether the transition actually fired.
203	export async function applyWithdraw(
204	  db: Transactable,
205	  id: string,
206	  decidedAt: Date,
207	): Promise<number> {
208	  const updated = await db
209	    .update(approvalRequests)
210	    .set({ status: "withdrawn", decidedAt, updatedAt: new Date() })
211	    .where(
212	      and(
213	        eq(approvalRequests.id, id),
214	        eq(approvalRequests.status, "pending"),
215	      ),
216	    )
217	    .returning({ id: approvalRequests.id })
218	  return updated.length
219	}
220	
221	// Companion mutator for the approve path — stamps `officialTier` and the
222	// matching `productLineId` (null for company tier) on the parent extension.
223	// Lives here (rather than in `extensions.ts`) because the approval
224	// orchestrator is the only legitimate writer; keeping the call adjacent to
225	// the request mutation makes that intent obvious. The DB CHECK enforces
226	// productLineId is non-null iff tier='productLine', so a bad pair from a
227	// caller is rejected at the boundary. Also clears the revocation audit
228	// fields in the same UPDATE — the invariant is "you're either currently
229	// Official with no pending revocation explanation, or Unofficial".
230	export async function setExtensionOfficialTier(
231	  db: Transactable,
232	  extensionId: string,
233	  tier: OfficialTier,
234	  productLineId: string | null,
235	): Promise<void> {
236	  await db
237	    .update(extensions)
238	    .set({
239	      officialTier: tier,
240	      productLineId,
241	      revokedAt: null,
242	      revokedByUserId: null,
243	      revocationNote: null,
244	      updatedAt: new Date(),
245	    })
246	    .where(eq(extensions.id, extensionId))
247	}
248	
249	// Companion mutator for the revoke path — atomically clears
250	// `officialTier` and `productLineId` while writing the audit trio
251	// (`revokedAt`, `revokedByUserId`, `revocationNote`). The orchestrator's
252	// pre-check already confirmed the row was Official; this method's
253	// returned affected-row count is the race-safe equivalent of a CAS,
254	// surfacing "someone else already revoked / re-approved" as a 0.
255	export interface RevocationPatch {
256	  revokedAt: Date
257	  revokedByUserId: string
258	  revocationNote: string
259	}
260	export async function applyRevocation(
261	  db: Transactable,
262	  extensionId: string,
263	  patch: RevocationPatch,
264	): Promise<number> {
265	  const updated = await db
266	    .update(extensions)
267	    .set({
268	      officialTier: null,
269	      productLineId: null,
270	      revokedAt: patch.revokedAt,
271	      revokedByUserId: patch.revokedByUserId,
272	      revocationNote: patch.revocationNote,
273	      updatedAt: new Date(),
274	    })
275	    .where(
276	      and(
277	        eq(extensions.id, extensionId),
278	        sql`${extensions.officialTier} IS NOT NULL`,
279	      ),
280	    )
281	    .returning({ id: extensions.id })
282	  return updated.length
283	}
284	
285	// Bulk lookup of `officialTier` for a set of extensions — used by the
286	// publisher dashboard to render the per-row badge alongside any pending
287	// requests in one round-trip.
288	export async function findTiersForExtensions(
289	  db: Transactable,
290	  extensionIds: string[],
291	): Promise<Map<string, OfficialTier | null>> {
292	  if (extensionIds.length === 0) return new Map()
293	  const rows = await db
294	    .select({ id: extensions.id, officialTier: extensions.officialTier })
295	    .from(extensions)
296	    .where(inArray(extensions.id, extensionIds))
297	  return new Map(rows.map((r) => [r.id, r.officialTier ?? null]))
298	}
299	
300	// Used by guards in `decide.post.ts` — confirms a user is assigned to the
301	// cell that the request originated from. Super-admins are handled in the
302	// orchestrator (a separate join) so this stays a pure reviewer check.
303	// ProductLineId is null for company-tier requests and the cell match must
304	// be exact on that NULLness to keep tiers from leaking into one another.
305	export async function isReviewerForCell(
306	  db: Transactable,
307	  userId: string,
308	  tier: OfficialTier,
309	  subCat: string,
310	  productLineId: string | null,
311	): Promise<boolean> {
312	  const [row] = await db
313	    .select({ id: approvalReviewers.id })
314	    .from(approvalReviewers)
315	    .where(
316	      and(
317	        eq(approvalReviewers.userId, userId),
318	        eq(approvalReviewers.tier, tier),
319	        eq(approvalReviewers.subCat, subCat),
320	        productLineId === null
321	          ? isNull(approvalReviewers.productLineId)
322	          : eq(approvalReviewers.productLineId, productLineId),
323	      ),
324	    )
325	    .limit(1)
326	  return !!row
327	}

1	import {
2	  isSuperAdmin,
3	  listCompanySubCatsForUser,
4	  listMatrix,
5	} from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
6	import { listAllProductLines } from "~~/server/repositories/productLines"
7	
8	// Returns the reviewer matrix plus per-cell `canEdit` annotations and the
9	// productLine list, so the admin UI can render the productLine grid and
10	// grey non-editable cells in one round-trip. The access gate widens from
11	// "super-admin only" to "super-admin OR any company-tier reviewer" — the
12	// non-super viewer sees the full read-only matrix and can only mutate
13	// productLine cells in their own subCats (see requireCellAdmin).
14	export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
15	  const user = await requireUser(event)
16	  const db = useDb()
17	  const [superAdmin, companySubCats] = await Promise.all([
18	    isSuperAdmin(db, user.id),
19	    listCompanySubCatsForUser(db, user.id),
20	  ])
21	  const isCompanyAdmin = companySubCats.length > 0
22	  if (!superAdmin && !isCompanyAdmin) {
23	    throw createError({ statusCode: 403, statusMessage: "Forbidden" })
24	  }
25	
26	  const editableSubCats = new Set(companySubCats)
27	  const [reviewers, productLines] = await Promise.all([
28	    listMatrix(db),
29	    listAllProductLines(db),
30	  ])
31	
32	  return {
33	    ok: true,
34	    productLines,
35	    reviewers: reviewers.map((r) => ({
36	      ...r,
37	      canEdit:
38	        superAdmin ||
39	        (r.tier === "productLine" && editableSubCats.has(r.subCat)),
40	    })),
41	    viewer: {
42	      isSuperAdmin: superAdmin,
43	      companySubCats,
44	    },
45	  }
46	})

1	import { insertReviewer } from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
2	import { AssignReviewerSchema } from "~~/shared/validators/approvals"
3	
4	// Per-cell authorisation: super-admins anywhere, company admins of subCat X
5	// may assign productLine reviewers for subCat X (any productLine), and
6	// company-tier cells stay super-admin-only. The validator's iff-rule
7	// already guarantees productLineId is present iff tier='productLine'.
8	export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
9	  const body = await readValidatedBody(event, (raw) =>
10	    AssignReviewerSchema.parse(raw),
11	  )
12	  await requireCellAdmin(event, {
13	    tier: body.tier,
14	    subCat: body.subCat,
15	    productLineId: body.productLineId ?? null,
16	  })
17	
18	  await insertReviewer(useDb(), {
19	    id: crypto.randomUUID(),
20	    tier: body.tier,
21	    subCat: body.subCat,
22	    productLineId: body.productLineId ?? null,
23	    userId: body.userId,
24	  })
25	  return { ok: true }
26	})

1	import {
2	  deleteReviewer,
3	  findReviewerById,
4	} from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
5	import { UnassignReviewerSchema } from "~~/shared/validators/approvals"
6	
7	// Load the row first so the cell-aware gate authorises against the row's
8	// own (tier, subCat, productLineId), not against anything the caller
9	// supplies. Without this, a company admin of subCat X could uncover the
10	// id of a company-tier reviewer in any subCat by guessing — the request
11	// body alone can't tell us which cell the id belongs to.
12	export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
13	  const body = await readValidatedBody(event, (raw) =>
14	    UnassignReviewerSchema.parse(raw),
15	  )
16	  const row = await findReviewerById(useDb(), body.id)
17	  if (!row) {
18	    throw createError({ statusCode: 404, statusMessage: "reviewer_not_found" })
19	  }
20	  await requireCellAdmin(event, {
21	    tier: row.tier,
22	    subCat: row.subCat,
23	    productLineId: row.productLineId,
24	  })
25	  await deleteReviewer(useDb(), body.id)
26	  return { ok: true }
27	})

message: "must be a key from FUNC_TAXONOMY",
// Product-line ids are seeded text PKs in kebab-case (wireless, datacom,
// terminals, cloud as of 0009). Validate the shape here; the DB FK + CHECK
// constraint enforces membership and the iff-rule against the actual tier.
+// Matrix admin assignment — five coordinates. Replaces the 3-coord
+// pre-redesign shape. Refines on top of the level/key keyspace and the
+//   - level='macro' → key is an l1 leaf
+//   - level='micro' → key is an l2 leaf
+// Both refines surface as Zod errors so the endpoint can render a useful
+// message instead of bouncing the raw 23514 from Postgres.
+        "categoryKey must be '*' for level=all, an l1 key for level=macro, or an l2 key for level=micro",
// "mine"  → requests this user submitted as a publisher
// Reviewer-queue narrowing keys. Reused from the cell-shape validators

-  // and the listing rail. No iff-rule between tier and productLineId here:

1	import {
2	  isSuperAdmin,
3	  listCellsForUser,
4	} from "~~/server/repositories/reviewers"
5	
6	// Lightweight "what can I do in the admin surface" probe — backs the
7	// require-reviewer / require-super-admin client middleware so the pages
8	// can fail fast with 403 → redirect rather than rendering and then
9	// hitting an empty page.
10	//
11	// Returns safe defaults for anonymous callers rather than 401, because
12	// the detail page renders for anonymous browsers too and needs to read
13	// this endpoint without bailing out — the gates on the actual admin
14	// pages still throw 401 separately. `cells` is returned alongside the
15	// role booleans so the approvals queue page can render smart pickers
16	// bounded to what the viewer actually covers; an empty array means "no
17	// cells" rather than "any cell allowed".
18	export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
19	  // Per-user, cookie-dependent payload — same caching contract as
20	  // /api/internal/auth/me.
21	  setResponseHeader(event, "Cache-Control", "private, no-store")
22	  setResponseHeader(event, "Vary", "Cookie")
23	  const user = await getSessionUser(event)
24	  if (!user) {
25	    return { isSuperAdmin: false, isReviewer: false, cells: [] }
26	  }
27	  const db = useDb()
28	  const [superAdmin, cells] = await Promise.all([
29	    isSuperAdmin(db, user.id),
30	    listCellsForUser(db, user.id),
31	  ])
32	  return {
33	    isSuperAdmin: superAdmin,
34	    isReviewer: superAdmin || cells.length > 0,
35	    cells,
36	  }
37	})

1	// @vitest-environment nuxt
2	import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest"
3	
4	// Mock the reviewers repo so we can dictate what isSuperAdmin returns.
5	vi.mock("~~/server/repositories/reviewers", () => ({
6	  isSuperAdmin: vi.fn(),
7	}))
8	
9	// Mock useDb so auth.ts doesn't reach for a real postgres connection.
10	vi.mock("./db", () => ({ useDb: () => ({ __tag: "db" }) }))
11	
12	// Mock better-auth so makeAuth() returns a stub whose getSession we can drive.
13	const getSessionMock = vi.fn()
14	vi.mock("better-auth", () => ({
15	  betterAuth: () => ({ api: { getSession: getSessionMock } }),
16	}))
17	vi.mock("better-auth/adapters/drizzle", () => ({
18	  drizzleAdapter: () => ({}),
19	}))
20	
21	const reviewersRepo = await import("~~/server/repositories/reviewers")
22	const { requireSuperAdmin } = await import("./auth")
23	
24	const FAKE_EVENT = {
25	  context: {},
26	  node: { req: { headers: {} } },
27	} as unknown as Parameters<typeof requireSuperAdmin>[0]
28	
29	beforeEach(() => {
30	  getSessionMock.mockReset()
31	  vi.mocked(reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin).mockReset()
32	})
33	
34	describe("requireSuperAdmin", () => {
35	  it("throws 401 Unauthenticated when there is no session", async () => {
36	    getSessionMock.mockResolvedValue(null)
37	    await expect(requireSuperAdmin(FAKE_EVENT)).rejects.toMatchObject({
38	      statusCode: 401,
39	    })
40	    // Short-circuits before touching the membership check.
41	    expect(reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
42	  })
43	
44	  it("throws 403 Forbidden when the session exists but the user is not a super-admin", async () => {
45	    getSessionMock.mockResolvedValue({ user: { id: "u-1", email: "a@x" } })
46	    vi.mocked(reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin).mockResolvedValue(false)
47	    await expect(requireSuperAdmin(FAKE_EVENT)).rejects.toMatchObject({
48	      statusCode: 403,
49	    })
50	    expect(reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
51	      expect.objectContaining({ __tag: "db" }),
52	      "u-1",
53	    )
54	  })
55	
56	  it("returns the session user when isSuperAdmin resolves true", async () => {
57	    const user = { id: "u-super", email: "amy@example.test" }
58	    getSessionMock.mockResolvedValue({ user })
59	    vi.mocked(reviewersRepo.isSuperAdmin).mockResolvedValue(true)
60	    const out = await requireSuperAdmin(FAKE_EVENT)
61	    expect(out).toEqual(user)
62	  })
63	})

2	
3	vi.mock("~~/server/repositories/approvals", () => ({
4	  insertRequest: vi.fn(),
5	  findById: vi.fn(),
6	  findPendingByExtension: vi.fn(),
7	  listForPublisher: vi.fn(),
8	  listPendingForCells: vi.fn(),
9	  applyDecision: vi.fn(),
10	  applyWithdraw: vi.fn(),
11	  setExtensionOfficialTier: vi.fn(),
12	  applyRevocation: vi.fn(),

14	}))
15	vi.mock("~~/server/repositories/reviewers", () => ({
16	  isSuperAdmin: vi.fn(),
17	  listCellsForUser: vi.fn(),
18	  listMatrix: vi.fn(),
19	}))
20	vi.mock("~~/server/repositories/extensions", () => ({
21	  findById: vi.fn(),
22	}))
23	
24	const TX = { __tag: "tx" } as const
25	const DB = {
26	  __tag: "db",
27	  transaction: vi.fn(async (cb: (tx: unknown) => Promise<unknown>) => cb(TX)),
28	} as const
29	
30	vi.mock("./db", () => ({ useDb: () => DB }))
31	
32	const sendEvent = vi.fn(async () => undefined)
33	// `safeSend` shares the same spy so existing `expect(sendEvent).…`
34	// assertions continue to observe approval-side event sends. The real
35	// `safeSend` wraps `inngest.send` in try/catch; the orchestrator's
36	// behavior under a rejected send is covered by the dedicated test below.
37	vi.mock("./inngest", () => ({
38	  inngest: { send: sendEvent },
39	  safeSend: async (event: unknown) => {
40	    try {
41	      await sendEvent(event as Parameters<typeof sendEvent>[0])
42	    } catch (error) {
43	      console.error("[inngest] safeSend failed", error)
44	    }
45	  },
46	}))
47	
48	const approvalsRepo = await import("~~/server/repositories/approvals")
49	const reviewersRepo = await import("~~/server/repositories/reviewers")
50	const extensionsRepo = await import("~~/server/repositories/extensions")
51	const {
52	  submitRequest,
53	  decideRequest,
54	  withdrawRequest,
55	  listReviewerQueue,
56	  revokeTier,
57	  ApprovalError,
58	} = await import("./approvals")
59	
60	// Minimal stand-in for `extensionsRepo.findById`'s detailSelect row — the
61	// orchestrator only reads publisherUserId, visibility, and id.
62	const EXT_PUBLISHED = {
63	  id: "ext-1",
64	  publisherUserId: "u-pub",
65	  visibility: "published" as const,
66	}
67	
68	const PENDING_ROW = {
69	  id: "req-1",
70	  extensionId: "ext-1",
71	  requestedTier: "company" as const,
72	  subCat: "softDev",
73	  productLineId: null as string | null,
74	  requestedByUserId: "u-pub",
75	  reason: null,
76	  status: "pending" as const,
77	  decidedByUserId: null,
78	  decidedAt: null,
79	  reviewerNote: null,
80	  createdAt: new Date(),

80	  createdAt: new Date(),
81	  updatedAt: new Date(),
82	}
83	
84	beforeEach(() => {
85	  vi.clearAllMocks()
86	  DB.transaction.mockImplementation(async (cb) => cb(TX))
87	})
88	
89	describe("submitRequest", () => {
90	  beforeEach(() => {
91	    vi.mocked(extensionsRepo.findById).mockResolvedValue(EXT_PUBLISHED as never)
92	    vi.mocked(approvalsRepo.findPendingByExtension).mockResolvedValue(null)
93	    vi.mocked(approvalsRepo.insertRequest).mockResolvedValue(PENDING_ROW)

content.subject: Commit 1: add taxonomy helpers + ancestor walk
content.description: shared/taxonomy.ts helpers (l2KeysFor, l1KeyFor, isL1Key, isL2Key, ALL_L1_KEYS, ALL_L2_KEYS, categoryAncestors) + tests. Standalone — no DB change.

content.subject: Commit 2: redesign matrix schema + rewrite all backend
content.description: Schema (approval_admins replacing approval_reviewers; approval_requests recreated with extension_category + l2 snapshots) + migration + ADR + CLAUDE.md + reviewers.ts → admins.ts rewrite + 5-coord requireCellAdmin + routing fan-out + endpoints accepting new 5-coord payload + AssignReviewerSchema refines + server-side tests.

content.subject: Commit 3: unify matrix UI + i18n + component tests
content.description: Collapse ReviewerMatrix{Company,ProductLine}.vue into one ext-type-toggled table; 3-tier vertical hierarchy with sticky All + collapsible macros + indented micros; per-cell canEdit; inheritance badges; 27 l2 i18n keys + new matrix strings (EN + ZH); component tests.

input.description: Find approval inserts in profile test

content.content:
75	
76	  beforeEach(async () => {
77	    await db.delete(approvalRequests)
78	  })
79	
80	  it("returns the user's requests with extension display fields joined in", async () => {
81	    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
82	      id: "req-1",
83	      extensionId: "ext-a",
84	      requestedTier: "company",
85	      subCat: "softDev",
86	      requestedByUserId: "u-pub",
87	      reason: "Lots of usage.",
88	      status: "pending",
89	    })
90	    const rows = await getRequestsForUser("u-pub")
91	    expect(rows).toHaveLength(1)
92	    expect(rows[0]).toMatchObject({
93	      requestId: "req-1",
94	      extensionId: "ext-a",
95	      slug: "ext-a",
96	      name: "Ext A",
97	      category: "skills",
98	      iconColor: "#abc",
99	      requestedTier: "company",
100	      subCat: "softDev",
101	      status: "pending",
102	      reason: "Lots of usage.",
103	      reviewerNote: null,
104	      decidedAt: null,
105	    })
106	    expect(rows[0]!.createdAt).toBeInstanceOf(Date)
107	  })
108	
109	  it("orders by createdAt desc — most recent submission first", async () => {
110	    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
111	      id: "req-old",
112	      extensionId: "ext-a",
113	      requestedTier: "productLine",
114	      subCat: "softDev",
115	      productLineId: "wireless",
116	      requestedByUserId: "u-pub",
117	      status: "pending",
118	    })
119	    await db
120	      .update(approvalRequests)
121	      .set({ createdAt: new Date("2026-06-01T10:00:00Z") })
122	      .where(approvalsIdEq("req-old"))
123	
124	    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
125	      id: "req-new",
126	      extensionId: "ext-b",
127	      requestedTier: "company",
128	      subCat: "docs",
129	      requestedByUserId: "u-pub",
130	      status: "pending",
131	    })
132	    await db
133	      .update(approvalRequests)
134	      .set({ createdAt: new Date("2026-06-05T10:00:00Z") })
135	      .where(approvalsIdEq("req-new"))
136	
137	    const rows = await getRequestsForUser("u-pub")
138	    expect(rows.map((r) => r.requestId)).toEqual(["req-new", "req-old"])
139	  })
140	
141	  it("does not leak another user's requests", async () => {
142	    await db.insert(approvalRequests).values({
143	      id: "req-other",
144	      extensionId: "ext-a",
145	      requestedTier: "company",
146	      subCat: "softDev",
147	      // Same extension, different requester.
148	      requestedByUserId: "u-other",
149	      status: "pending",
150	    })
151	    const rows = await getRequestsForUser("u-pub")
152	    expect(rows).toHaveLength(0)
153	  })
154	
155	  it("returns empty for a user with no requests", async () => {
156	    const rows = await getRequestsForUser("u-pub")
157	    expect(rows).toEqual([])
158	  })
159	})

content.subject: Commit 1: add taxonomy helpers + ancestor walk
content.description: shared/taxonomy.ts helpers (l2KeysFor, l1KeyFor, isL1Key, isL2Key, ALL_L1_KEYS, ALL_L2_KEYS, categoryAncestors) + tests. Standalone — no DB change.

content.subject: Commit 2: redesign matrix schema + rewrite all backend
content.description: Schema (approval_admins replacing approval_reviewers; approval_requests recreated with extension_category + l2 snapshots) + migration + ADR + CLAUDE.md + reviewers.ts → admins.ts rewrite + 5-coord requireCellAdmin + routing fan-out + endpoints accepting new 5-coord payload + AssignReviewerSchema refines + server-side tests.

content.subject: Commit 3: unify matrix UI + i18n + component tests
content.description: Collapse ReviewerMatrix{Company,ProductLine}.vue into one ext-type-toggled table; 3-tier vertical hierarchy with sticky All + collapsible macros + indented micros; per-cell canEdit; inheritance badges; 27 l2 i18n keys + new matrix strings (EN + ZH); component tests.

input.old_string: ## Update 2026-06-09 — tier revocation
input.new_string:
## Update 2026-06-09b — matrix redesign (ext-type × 3-tier × column)

The 2026-06-08 + revocation updates left the matrix at 45 cells —
`9 subCats × (Company + 4 ProductLines)` — with a flat one-level
category axis (FUNC_TAXONOMY l1 only) and exact-match routing. Two
limitations surfaced in practice:

1. **No per-extension-type split**. A Skills reviewer and an MCP
   reviewer overlap if they care about the same subCat, but they
   review fundamentally different artifacts. The matrix had no way
   to route them separately.
2. **Category granularity was either too coarse (l1 only) or
   inexpressive (l2 was stored on the extension but ignored by
   routing)**. A reviewer who only knows `reqAnalysis` couldn't sign
   up just for that leaf; they had to take the whole `systemDesign`.

Refinements (do not revoke the four locked decisions; they remain
accurate):

- **Decision #1 widens to 5 coordinates**: routing key is now
  `(extensionCategory × tier × productLineId? × categoryLevel × categoryKey)`.
  `extensionCategory` reuses the existing `extension_category` enum
  (`skills | mcp | slash | plugins`); `categoryLevel` is a new
  `admin_category_level` enum (`all | macro | micro`); `categoryKey`
  is `'*'` at the `all` level, an l1 leaf at `macro`, an l2 leaf at
  `micro`. The reviewer table is renamed `approval_admins` because a
  row now grants both review duty AND admin authority over its
  covered shadow — see the cover relation below.
- **Decision #4 widens via a 2-D cover relation**. Matrix edits are
  gated by `requireCellAdmin(event, cell)` which returns true iff
  the caller holds an admin row whose covered shadow includes the
  target cell:
    - **Column-tier dim** — `(company, null) ⊇ (productLine, X)` for
      any X. A company admin can configure product-line admins. A
      PL admin can only configure same-PL cells.
    - **Category dim** — `(all, '*')` covers everything; `(macro, l1)`
      covers itself + its 3 l2 children; `(micro, l2)` covers only
      itself.
  Cross-extensionCategory authority does NOT exist — each ext-type
  matrix is independent. Super-admins still bypass the gate.
- **Routing is fan-out on the category dim, EXACT on column-tier**.
  A pending request at `(E, T, C, l1, l2)` routes to any admin whose
  `(extensionCategory, tier, productLineId)` matches exactly AND
  whose `(categoryLevel, categoryKey)` covers the request's
  `(macro=l1, micro=l2)` via the ancestor walk. Crucially, a Company
  admin does NOT review Product-Line requests — the company →
  product-line widening applies to admin authority only. Routing is
  implemented as a single JOIN in
  `approvalsRepo.listPendingForUser`; super-admins read
  `listAllPending` instead. Request rows snapshot `extensionCategory`
  and `l2` at submission time so the JOIN doesn't need a hop through
  `extensions`.
- **`approval_requests` columns added**: `extension_category` (NOT
  NULL, mirrors `extensions.category` at submission time) and `l2`
  (nullable — extensions without an l2 classification still submit;
  routing fan-out simply omits the `micro` candidate for those).
  Updated `idx_approval_status_cell` carries both.

Implementation notes:

- **Clean replacement migration** (`0012_matrix_redesign.sql`): drops
  `approval_reviewers` and `approval_requests`, recreates both under
  the new shape. Pre-launch product per CLAUDE.md — current cells
  are seed data, so no backfill story is owed.
- **Partial unique indexes on `approval_admins`**: two indexes, one
  per tier (`approval_admins_pl_uq` and `approval_admins_co_uq`),
  each carrying exactly the columns that participate. Same pattern
  as the 2026-06-08 indexes, just widened to the 5-coord key.
- **`/api/v1` contract is unchanged.** The CLI still sees `badge:
  "official"` derived from `officialTier != null`; extensionCategory
  is already exposed on extension rows; l2 is not surfaced. A future
  v2 break could expose the new fields; until then the CLI sees
  nothing new.
- **Auth gate single-query**: `findCoveringAdmin` builds the cover
  relation in code (≤2 column shapes × ≤3 category shapes = ≤6 cell
  candidates), then ORs them into one indexed probe on
  `idx_admins_user`. Same approach as `isAdminCoveringRequest` for
  the decide-path gate, so coverage logic lives in one place.
- **JOIN-driven queue**: `listPendingForUser` is a single SQL
  statement that expresses the cover relation directly. `DISTINCT`
  on `r.id` de-dupes when the same user holds two admin rows that
  both cover a request — the same dedup pattern as today's
  `listReviewerQueue` Set, just promoted into SQL.

What we gave up:

- The l2 keyspace doubles the row count of any admin who wants
  micro-level granularity. We accept this — the matrix UI groups
  micros under their macros (`shared/taxonomy.ts:l2KeysFor`) so a
  full Skills × Wireless reviewer at the l2 level is 27 explicit
  rows, not the implicit 1.
- Routing's exact-match on column-tier means a Company admin who
  also wants to be in the PL request queue must hold both an admin
  row at Company AND one at each PL they care about. We accept this
  because the alternative — implicit Company → PL routing — would
  silently aggregate company admins into every PL queue, which read
  worse in early prototyping than the explicit assignment.

Cross-references for this update:

- Migration: `drizzle/0012_matrix_redesign.sql`
- Schema: `shared/db/schema/approval.ts` (approvalAdmins,
  approvalRequests with extensionCategory + l2),
  `shared/taxonomy.ts` (l1/l2 helpers + `categoryAncestors`)
- Repository rename: `server/repositories/admins.ts` (was `reviewers.ts`)
- Auth: `server/utils/auth.ts:requireCellAdmin` (5-coord)
- Orchestrator: `server/utils/approvals.ts` (submit snapshots
  category + l2; decide path uses `isAdminCoveringRequest`; queue
  uses `listPendingForUser`/`listAllPending`)
- Validators: `shared/validators/approvals.ts:AssignAdminSchema`
- UI: `app/components/approvals/ReviewerMatrix.vue` (unified table
  with ext-type toggle + 3-tier rows — lands in the follow-up commit)

50	- When a binding decision changes, update `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.
51	- **No agent-attribution noise in commits or PRs.** Do not add a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer (e.g. `Co-Authored-By: Claude …`) to commit messages, and do not append the "🤖 Generated with Claude Code" footer to PR descriptions. The branch already records authorship and the content speaks for itself.
52	- **Dev sign-in.** `bun run db:seed` plants six demo users on `*@agentcenter.dev` (`amy` = super-admin, the rest publishers / reviewers — see `scripts/seed.ts:CREATORS`). The shared password comes from the `SEED_PASSWORD` env var; the seed fails fast if it isn't set so a misconfigured environment can't hand out a predictable default. Run as `SEED_PASSWORD=anything-you-like bun run db:seed`. Rotate by re-running with a new env value; the deterministic credential row id makes it idempotent.
53	
54	## Project structure
55	
56	See `docs/plan.md` §1 for the full tree.
57	
58	- `app/pages/...` — file-based routes (locale-prefixed via `@nuxtjs/i18n`)
59	- `app/components/{ui,layout,extension,filters,publish,profile}` — Vue components
60	- `app/composables/`, `app/middleware/` — client state + route guards
61	- `server/api/v1/...` — public registry API consumed by the CLI (frozen contract)
62	- `server/api/internal/...` — form-backing endpoints (Nuxt's analogue of Next.js server actions)
63	- `server/api/{auth,upload,inngest}/...` — Better Auth, upload signing, Inngest webhook
64	- `server/utils/` — server-side singletons and queries
65	- `shared/{db/schema,validators,search,tags,taxonomy,data,types}` — code reachable from both `app/` and `server/`
66	- `cli/` — separate Bun-built binary
67	- `drizzle/` — generated migrations
68	- `docs/` — plan, ADRs, manifest spec

file.content:
- When a binding decision changes, update `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.
- **No agent-attribution noise in commits or PRs.** Do not add a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer (e.g. `Co-Authored-By: Claude …`) to commit messages, and do not append the "🤖 Generated with Claude Code" footer to PR descriptions. The branch already records authorship and the content speaks for itself.
- **Dev sign-in.** `bun run db:seed` plants six demo users on `*@agentcenter.dev` (`amy` = super-admin, the rest publishers / reviewers — see `scripts/seed.ts:CREATORS`). The shared password comes from the `SEED_PASSWORD` env var; the seed fails fast if it isn't set so a misconfigured environment can't hand out a predictable default. Run as `SEED_PASSWORD=anything-you-like bun run db:seed`. Rotate by re-running with a new env value; the deterministic credential row id makes it idempotent.

## Project structure

See `docs/plan.md` §1 for the full tree.

- `app/pages/...` — file-based routes (locale-prefixed via `@nuxtjs/i18n`)
- `app/components/{ui,layout,extension,filters,publish,profile}` — Vue components
- `app/composables/`, `app/middleware/` — client state + route guards
- `server/api/v1/...` — public registry API consumed by the CLI (frozen contract)
- `server/api/internal/...` — form-backing endpoints (Nuxt's analogue of Next.js server actions)
- `server/api/{auth,upload,inngest}/...` — Better Auth, upload signing, Inngest webhook
- `server/utils/` — server-side singletons and queries
- `shared/{db/schema,validators,search,tags,taxonomy,data,types}` — code reachable from both `app/` and `server/`
- `cli/` — separate Bun-built binary
- `drizzle/` — generated migrations
- `docs/` — plan, ADRs, manifest spec

input.old_string: 12. **Official-tier approval workflow**: extensions default to Unofficial; elevation to Product-Line Official or Company Official goes through a reviewer matrix indexed by `(tier × FUNC_TAXONOMY subCat)`, additionally keyed by `productLine` (data-driven `product_lines` table; current rows: wireless, datacom, terminals, cloud) for the `productLine` tier only. Tiers are independent (no PL→Company ladder), `officialTier` lives in its own enum column, and the `/api/v1` `badge` field is read-derived from it to preserve the frozen CLI contract. Matrix edits: super-admins everywhere; company-tier admins of a given subCat may manage productLine-tier admins for the same subCat. Revocation is a super-admin action from the detail page; revocation metadata (timestamp / admin / required note) lives on `extensions` and clears on the next approval. Full rationale and trade-offs in `docs/adr/0001-official-tier-approval-workflow.md` (including the 2026-06-08 and 2026-06-09 updates).
input.new_string: 12. **Official-tier approval workflow**: extensions default to Unofficial; elevation to Product-Line Official or Company Official goes through an admin matrix indexed by 5 coords — `(extensionCategory × tier × productLineId? × categoryLevel × categoryKey)`. `extensionCategory` reuses the existing `extension_category` enum so the matrix is tabbed per ext-type; `categoryLevel` is `all | macro | micro` (wildcard / FUNC_TAXONOMY l1 / l2); `productLineId` participates only for the `productLine` tier. Tiers are independent (no PL→Company ladder), `officialTier` lives in its own enum column, and the `/api/v1` `badge` field is read-derived from it to preserve the frozen CLI contract. Matrix edits use a 2-D cover relation: super-admins everywhere; any user whose admin row covers the target cell on both the column-tier axis (`(company, null) ⊇ (productLine, X)`) and the category axis (`all ⊇ macro ⊇ micro`) within the same extensionCategory. Routing is fan-out on the category dim and EXACT on column-tier (company admins do NOT receive product-line requests for review). Revocation is a super-admin action from the detail page; revocation metadata (timestamp / admin / required note) lives on `extensions` and clears on the next approval. Full rationale and trade-offs in `docs/adr/0001-official-tier-approval-workflow.md` (including the 2026-06-08, 2026-06-09, and 2026-06-09b updates).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# AgentCenter (Nuxt)

Marketplace for AI agent extensions (Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins) with internal-enterprise features (departments, scope, tags, collections) and a companion CLI for actual machine-side install. Bilingual (EN/ZH) from day one.

This is a rewrite of the original Next.js implementation in Nuxt 4. The product surface and the public `/api/v1` contract are preserved verbatim so the existing CLI keeps working.

See `docs/plan.md` for the full implementation plan, milestone schedule, validate pipeline, coverage thresholds, layer boundaries, and detailed commit/PR rules.

## Tech stack

- TypeScript, Nuxt 4 (Nitro server, Vue 3, Vite), Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn-vue + Reka UI
- Bun for local dev, scripts, and CLI build; Node runtime in production
- PostgreSQL (Supabase by default; any Postgres via `DATABASE_URL`) with Drizzle ORM (`drizzle-orm/postgres-js`)
- Better Auth (cookie sessions, Drizzle adapter); CLI uses device-code flow on the same `sessions` table
- Supabase Storage for bundle storage (default); R2 via S3 SDK supported as alternative
- Inngest for background jobs (scan, index, publish)
- `@nuxtjs/i18n` with `strategy: 'prefix'`; locales `en` (default) + `zh`, always-prefixed URLs
- Fonts via `@nuxt/fonts`: Inter (UI), Fraunces (display, italic), JetBrains Mono (tags/counts)
- Postgres FTS + `pg_trgm` for search; Meilisearch deferred
- vee-validate + Zod for forms
- markdown-it + DOMPurify for README rendering (server-side)
- Pinia (`@pinia/nuxt`) only where cross-component client state is needed

## Locked product decisions

These mirror the original v1 product decisions. Same numbering; do not revisit inside a normal PR. If a PR needs to change one, update this section and `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.

1. **Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI** for v1.
2. **Themes**: Editorial Ivory (default) + Dark + Mono Clean. Selectable via the TopBar theme picker; persisted in a `theme` cookie.
3. **Filter UI**: single-row quiet pill rail (revised 2026-05-21, replacing the earlier "Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only" decision). Scope pills + Dept/Creator/Publisher pickers + Tag-drawer trigger live inline on one row of compact bordered pills with no `border-b` divider below. The Tag drawer's expanded panel still wraps to a new row when opened — drawer behavior is retained for tag *selection* only, not for the rail as a whole.
4. **Sign-up**: open. SSO/invite-only later if needed.
5. **Locale URLs**: always prefixed; no implicit default.
6. **Detail page README**: raw markdown stored, server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify. Manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) rendered as a side panel.
7. **Install button**: triggers real install via the Agent CLI. Web records install events; the CLI does the work.
8. **Agent CLI**: agent-agnostic with Claude-first defaults. Extensions declare destination paths per agent in their manifest; user can override via `agentcenter config set`. Default target: `~/.claude/...`. Distributed via npm.
9. **Department IDs**: dotted-path text PKs (e.g. `eng.cloud.infra`). Descendant filter is a single `LIKE 'parent.%'` predicate, not a recursive CTE.
10. **Dynamic content i18n**: column-per-language (`name` + `nameZh`, `description` + `descriptionZh`). `tags` table carries `labelEn` + `labelZh`.
11. **Accent usage**: `--color-accent` is reserved for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action. Secondary active states (sidebar nav, filter trigger pills, non-primary picker chips) use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight, not tinted fills. The home should carry at most two accent moments per viewport; the goal is "one intentional accent moment" wherever practical. Auth pages, error states, and UI primitive focus rings are exempt.
12. **Official-tier approval workflow**: extensions default to Unofficial; elevation to Product-Line Official or Company Official goes through a reviewer matrix indexed by `(tier × FUNC_TAXONOMY subCat)`, additionally keyed by `productLine` (data-driven `product_lines` table; current rows: wireless, datacom, terminals, cloud) for the `productLine` tier only. Tiers are independent (no PL→Company ladder), `officialTier` lives in its own enum column, and the `/api/v1` `badge` field is read-derived from it to preserve the frozen CLI contract. Matrix edits: super-admins everywhere; company-tier admins of a given subCat may manage productLine-tier admins for the same subCat. Revocation is a super-admin action from the detail page; revocation metadata (timestamp / admin / required note) lives on `extensions` and clears on the next approval. Full rationale and trade-offs in `docs/adr/0001-official-tier-approval-workflow.md` (including the 2026-06-08 and 2026-06-09 updates).

Nuxt-specific technical decisions (deployment target, auth choice, DB driver, storage default, public API contract, validate pipeline, coverage thresholds, layer boundaries) live in `docs/plan.md` so they only have to be maintained in one place.

## Workflow

- **Commit per coherent unit** — usually one or several commits per phase, split where natural (schema vs. consumers, refactor vs. feature, deps vs. first use). Conventional Commits with phase scope: `feat(p2-db): add extension drizzle schema`. See `docs/plan.md` §13 for granularity calibration and the per-commit vs. per-PR validate split.
- **Pause at each phase boundary** for an explicit human checkpoint before starting the next phase. Never run `git push` without an explicit request.
- **No `--amend` once pushed, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.
- **Breaking changes to `/api/v1`** require `!` after the scope and a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
- Always work on a feature branch and open a PR — never commit to `main` directly.
- When a binding decision changes, update `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.
- **No agent-attribution noise in commits or PRs.** Do not add a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer (e.g. `Co-Authored-By: Claude …`) to commit messages, and do not append the "🤖 Generated with Claude Code" footer to PR descriptions. The branch already records authorship and the content speaks for itself.
- **Dev sign-in.** `bun run db:seed` plants six demo users on `*@agentcenter.dev` (`amy` = super-admin, the rest publishers / reviewers — see `scripts/seed.ts:CREATORS`). The shared password comes from the `SEED_PASSWORD` env var; the seed fails fast if it isn't set so a misconfigured environment can't hand out a predictable default. Run as `SEED_PASSWORD=anything-you-like bun run db:seed`. Rotate by re-running with a new env value; the deterministic credential row id makes it idempotent.

## Project structure

See `docs/plan.md` §1 for the full tree.

- `app/pages/...` — file-based routes (locale-prefixed via `@nuxtjs/i18n`)
- `app/components/{ui,layout,extension,filters,publish,profile}` — Vue components
- `app/composables/`, `app/middleware/` — client state + route guards
- `server/api/v1/...` — public registry API consumed by the CLI (frozen contract)
- `server/api/internal/...` — form-backing endpoints (Nuxt's analogue of Next.js server actions)
- `server/api/{auth,upload,inngest}/...` — Better Auth, upload signing, Inngest webhook
- `server/utils/` — server-side singletons and queries
- `shared/{db/schema,validators,search,tags,taxonomy,data,types}` — code reachable from both `app/` and `server/`
- `cli/` — separate Bun-built binary
- `drizzle/` — generated migrations
- `docs/` — plan, ADRs, manifest spec

content.subject: Commit 3: unify matrix UI + i18n + component tests
content.description: Collapse ReviewerMatrix{Company,ProductLine}.vue into one ext-type-toggled table; 3-tier vertical hierarchy with sticky All + collapsible macros + indented micros; per-cell canEdit; inheritance badges; 27 l2 i18n keys + new matrix strings (EN + ZH); component tests.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-approvals): redesign matrix to ext-type × 3-tier × column

Reframes the approval admin matrix per the 2026-06-09b ADR-0001
update. The cell key widens from `(tier × subCat × productLineId?)`
to a five-dimensional shape:

  (extensionCategory × tier × productLineId? × categoryLevel × categoryKey)

extensionCategory reuses the existing `extension_category` enum so
the matrix is tabbed per ext-type. categoryLevel is a new enum
(`all | macro | micro`) — `all` is the wildcard root, `macro` is a
FUNC_TAXONOMY l1 leaf, `micro` is an l2 leaf. categoryKey is `'*'`
at level `all`, an l1 key at `macro`, an l2 key at `micro`.

A row in the renamed `approval_admins` table now grants both review
duty AND admin authority over its covered shadow, so the table name
follows. The cover relation has two independent axes:

  - column-tier:  (company, null) ⊇ (productLine, X) ⊇ self
  - category:     (all, '*') ⊇ (macro, l1) ⊇ (micro, l2-under-l1)

`requireCellAdmin` is the single gate for matrix edits — it returns
true iff the caller holds an admin row whose covered shadow includes
the target cell (within the same extensionCategory). Cross-ext-type
authority is intentionally absent. Routing for the reviewer queue
fans out on the category dim only; column-tier matches exactly, so
company admins do NOT pick up product-line requests for review (the
company → PL widening is admin authority only). Implemented as a
single JOIN in `approvalsRepo.listPendingForUser`.

Request rows snapshot `extension_category` and `l2` at submission
time so the routing JOIN doesn't have to hop through `extensions`,
matching the existing precedent for `requested_tier` and `sub_cat`.

Migration 0012 is a clean drop+recreate of both `approval_requests`
and `approval_reviewers` (renamed to `approval_admins`) — pre-launch
product per CLAUDE.md, so the existing matrix is seed data and no
backfill story is owed. The admin matrix UI page is stubbed to a
flat list for this commit; the unified grid (ext-type toggle +
3-tier rows + per-cell canEdit) lands in the next commit.

ADR-0001 gains a 2026-06-09b update section; CLAUDE.md §12 reflects
the new 5-coord shape and the cover-relation wording.
EOF
)"

18	})

32	  }),

36	      modelValue: { type: String, default: "" },

content.text: Writing the new unified ReviewerMatrix.vue.

content.content: The file /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/app/components/approvals/ReviewerMatrix.vue has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -// Tabbed shell over the two tier-specific matrix views.
-// Product-Line tab — (subCat × productLine) grid. Edits permitted for
-//   super-admins anywhere and for company admins of the row's subCat
-// The active tab persists in the URL hash so deep links survive a reload.
+// Redesigned matrix shell — see ADR-0001 (2026-06-09b). The vertical axis
+// is a 3-tier hierarchy (All → Macro = FUNC_TAXONOMY l1 → Micro = l2);
+// the horizontal axis fuses Company and the product lines into one
+// table; and the previous Tier tabs become an `extension_category`
+// toggle. Per-cell `canEdit` flows from the API based on the 2-D cover
+// relation; non-editable cells render the chip controls in a disabled
+// state and skip the "+ Add" tile.
-// Reverse binding so browser back / forward (or any external hash change)
-// keeps the UI in sync with the URL. Guard against the loop the assignment
-// would otherwise trigger via the watcher above.
-// Provide a Ref<boolean> for the subviews so they don't need to thread
-// the viewer prop themselves. Wrapped as a computed so reactivity follows
-// the prop without an explicit re-broadcast.
+// Columns: a fixed "Company" column first, then product lines in
+// Macro rows (one per FUNC_TAXONOMY l1 leaf), grouped visually by their
+// funcCat parent for a colour hint along the leading edge.
+// `expanded[macroKey] = true` reveals the three micro rows nested under
+// it. The toggle uses a chevron — collapsed is the default to keep the
+// Index admins by composite cell key — one Map keyed by
+// `cat|tier|pl|level|cKey`. Faster than re-scanning the full array per
+// cell and keeps the template terse.
+// A cell that has no admin rows still needs to know if the viewer is
+// allowed to add one — `coveringCells` carries that signal. Mirrors
+// `findCoveringAdmin` on the server: same extensionCategory + cover on
+// both axes. Centralising the rule here keeps the chip dialog and the
+// l2 → l1 map for the cover walk above. The wider helper lives in
+// `shared/taxonomy.ts` (`l1KeyFor`); we materialise the lookup once here
+// to avoid the function-call overhead inside the template.
+      for (const l2 of leaf.l2) map.set(l2, leaf.key)
+// Picker dialog state — one dialog shared across every "+ Add" tile.
+    <!-- Extension-category toggle (4 buttons). Replaces the pre-redesign
class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 rounded-lg border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) p-1"
+              class="sticky left-0 z-10 bg-(--color-bg) border-b border-(--color-border) py-2 px-3 text-left text-xs font-semibold text-(--color-ink-muted)"
+              class="border-b border-(--color-border) py-2 px-3 text-left text-xs font-semibold text-(--color-ink)"
+          <!-- All row (wildcard, single sticky row at the top). -->
+              class="sticky left-0 z-10 bg-(--color-bg) border-b border-(--color-border) py-3 px-3 text-left font-semibold text-(--color-ink)"
+                  class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 rounded-full border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) px-2 py-0.5 text-xs"
+                    class="rounded-full border border-dashed border-(--color-border) px-2 py-0.5 text-xs text-(--color-ink-muted) hover:bg-(--color-sidebar)/60"
+          <!-- Macro rows. Each has a chevron that toggles its three micro
+               children. The micro rows stay rendered in the DOM only when
+               their parent is expanded — keeps the page light on first load. -->
+                class="sticky left-0 z-10 bg-(--color-bg) border-b border-(--color-border) py-3 px-3 text-left font-medium text-(--color-ink)"
+                    class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 rounded-full border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) px-2 py-0.5 text-xs"
+                      class="rounded-full border border-dashed border-(--color-border) px-2 py-0.5 text-xs text-(--color-ink-muted) hover:bg-(--color-sidebar)/60"
+                class="sticky left-0 z-10 bg-(--color-bg) border-b border-(--color-border) py-2 pl-9 pr-3 text-left text-sm text-(--color-ink-muted)"
+                    class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 rounded-full border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) px-2 py-0.5 text-xs"
+                      class="rounded-full border border-dashed border-(--color-border) px-2 py-0.5 text-xs text-(--color-ink-muted) hover:bg-(--color-sidebar)/60"
+            class="inline-flex items-center gap-2 rounded-md bg-(--color-accent) px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-semibold text-(--color-accent-fg) hover:opacity-90 disabled:opacity-50"

content.subject: Commit 2: redesign matrix schema + rewrite all backend
content.description: Schema (approval_admins replacing approval_reviewers; approval_requests recreated with extension_category + l2 snapshots) + migration + ADR + CLAUDE.md + reviewers.ts → admins.ts rewrite + 5-coord requireCellAdmin + routing fan-out + endpoints accepting new 5-coord payload + AssignReviewerSchema refines + server-side tests.

content.subject: Commit 3: unify matrix UI + i18n + component tests
content.description: Collapse ReviewerMatrix{Company,ProductLine}.vue into one ext-type-toggled table; 3-tier vertical hierarchy with sticky All + collapsible macros + indented micros; per-cell canEdit; inheritance badges; 27 l2 i18n keys + new matrix strings (EN + ZH); component tests.

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(p9-approvals): unify reviewer matrix into one ext-type-toggled table

Collapses the pre-redesign Company/Product-Line tab pair into a single
5-column grid (Company + 4 product lines) with an `extension_category`
segmented control at the top (Skills / MCP / Slash / Plugins). The
vertical axis is the 3-tier hierarchy from ADR-0001's 2026-06-09b
update:

  All  (sticky wildcard row)
   └── 9 macro rows (FUNC_TAXONOMY l1, collapsible)
        └── 3 micro rows each (FUNC_TAXONOMY l2, indented)

Each cell renders the admin chips at exactly its (level, key, tier,
productLineId) coordinates. Per-cell `canEdit` comes from the API
annotation; cells the viewer's `coveringCells` don't reach hide the
"+ Add" tile entirely. The same dialog state drives every "+ Add"
button — assignment posts the full 5-coord payload that the redesigned
endpoint expects.

ReviewerMatrixCompany.vue and ReviewerMatrixProductLine.vue are
deleted — their chip / dialog pattern is lifted into the unified
component. The active extension category persists in the URL hash
(`#cat=skills`), mirroring the pre-redesign `#tab=` pattern.

i18n: matrix strings widen to the new shape (`cat.*`, `level.*`,
`addSubtitleUnified`, `categoryAxisLabel`); the old `tabs.{company,
productLine}` keys are removed. EN + ZH stay in lockstep. The l1/l2
taxonomy keys consumed elsewhere (publish wizard, SubCatPicker) were
already present and need no changes.

ReviewerMatrix.nuxt.test.ts is rewritten to cover the new shape:
ext-type filtering, the All row + 9 collapsible macros, micro reveal,
5-coord assign payload, viewer.coveringCells gating, and the remove
path.
EOF
)"
